summary: Matched the first 46.47% of src/melee/gr/grmaterial.c by adding grMaterial material/JObj traversal helpers, item wrapper utilities, an HSD_MObjInfo class initializer, and one missing it_2E6A item prototype. The PR also converted the corresponding scratches.txt entries from 100% scratch status to OK for functions 801C87D0 through 801C8E74. No review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Created substantive C implementation content for src/melee/gr/grmaterial.c, growing it from an effectively empty file to 217 lines.
Added includes for lb/forward.h, baselib forward/types, it_266F, item, it_2E6A, and baselib dobj/gobj/id/jobj support.
Defined HSD_MObjInfo grMaterial_803E0A20 and a 35-entry data_section_pad array.
Added material flag helpers grMaterial_801C87D0 and grMaterial_801C897C that iterate a JObj's DObj list and call HSD_MObjSetFlags or HSD_MObjClearFlags on each non-null MObj.
Added recursive tree-wide wrappers grMaterial_801C8858 and grMaterial_801C8A04 to set or clear material flags over a JObj hierarchy.
Added grMaterial_801C8B28 to walk from a JObj to its root parent.
Added grMaterial_801C8B68, a recursive JObj traversal that conditionally inserts JObjs into the HSD ID table based on flags and skips traversal when flag 0x1000 is set.

Added several item-facing wrappers: grMaterial_801C8CDC calls Item_8026A8EC; grMaterial_801C8CFC and grMaterial_801C8D44 call it_802E6AEC with different argument layouts; grMaterial_801C8D98 calls it_802725D4 then Item_80268E5C; grMaterial_801C8DE0 updates an item's hurt capsule offsets and scale; grMaterial_801C8E08 calls it_802756E0; grMaterial_801C8E48 checks whether x52C_item_script is NULL; grMaterial_801C8E68 assigns ground_or_air.

Added grMaterial_801C8E74 to initialize grMaterial_803E0A20 as a subclass-like MObj info record using hsdInitClassInfo, copying release/amnesia/load/make_texp behavior and assigning setup to fn_801C8EF8.
Added the missing prototype for it_802E6AEC to src/melee/it/items/it_2E6A.h.

Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt entries for func_801C87D0, func_801C8858, func_801C897C, func_801C8A04, func_801C8B28, func_801C8B68, func_801C8CDC, func_801C8CFC, func_801C8D44, func_801C8D98, func_801C8DE0, func_801C8E08, func_801C8E48, func_801C8E68, and func_801C8E74 from 100% to OK.
smart_moves: Reused existing baselib traversal APIs such as HSD_JObjGetDObj, HSD_JObjGetParent, HSD_JObjGetChild, HSD_JObjGetNext, HSD_MObjSetFlags, and HSD_MObjClearFlags instead of open-coding unrelated structure assumptions beyond what was needed for matching.
Factored single-node material flag mutation into grMaterial_801C87D0 and grMaterial_801C897C, then reused those helpers in recursive hierarchy functions.

Kept null-guard patterns and redundant-looking conditional expressions such as dobj != NULL ? dobj->mobj : NULL and jobj == NULL ? NULL : jobj->child, which likely preserved compiler output for matching.
Preserved explicit unused stack locals/arrays in small traversal functions, indicating matching was prioritized over source cleanliness.
Added only the needed external item prototype it_802E6AEC to the item header, enabling grmaterial.c to call it without broader header churn.
Converted scratch entries to OK after landing matched source, keeping the scratch database aligned with decompiled code.
decomp_lessons: For grmaterial-style JObj/DObj/MObj traversal, matching may require retaining redundant null checks and temporary locals even when they are logically unnecessary.
Recursive hierarchy operations can be split into single-node helpers plus recursive wrappers, mirroring the assembly structure while improving source readability.
When decompiling code that bridges ground/material systems and items, expect to add narrowly scoped item prototypes rather than pulling in large unrelated declarations.
Class-info initialization for HSD objects may require copying function pointers from a base info object after hsdInitClassInfo and then overriding only the custom callbacks.
Scratch status should be changed from 100% to OK when the corresponding functions are represented in committed source.
Simple wrapper functions are valuable decomp progress: many matched grMaterial functions are thin calls into existing item or HSD APIs.
naming_conventions: New grmaterial functions use the project style grMaterial_XXXXXXXX for address-based names once moved from generic func_XXXXXXXX scratches into source.
The file-local forward declaration for the unmatched setup callback keeps the fn_801C8EF8 name, indicating unresolved/static callbacks may retain fn_ address naming.
The global class info object is named grMaterial_803E0A20, following system/address naming for data symbols.
Item and HSD symbols retain established project prefixes such as Item_, it_, HSD_, hsdMObj, and hsdInitClassInfo.
The newly exposed item function prototype uses the address-based name it_802E6AEC and an address comment /* 2E6AEC */ in header style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit unused locals such as u32 unused and u32 unused[8] to shape stack frame/layout for matched output.
Retained redundant ternary null checks around dobj and jobj access to preserve expected codegen.
Included a strange no-op expression !arg1; inside grMaterial_801C8B68, strongly suggesting source was arranged to match assembly rather than for semantic clarity.
Kept address-based function names and scratch alignment for a batch of small functions to make matching progress traceable.
Added data_section_pad[35] beside grMaterial_803E0A20, suggesting data-section layout/padding was required for matching the file's data region.
Used direct field access for Item hurt capsule fields and JObj flags where needed to match low-level behavior.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete the remaining unmatched grmaterial functions fn_801C8EF8, grMaterial_801C92C0, grMaterial_801C94D8, grMaterial_801C9604, grMaterial_801C9698, grMaterial_801C9490, grMaterial_801C95C4, fn_801C9664, grMaterial_801C8E28, and grMaterial_801C9470.
Search for later cleanup or renaming of grMaterial_801C8B68, especially the no-op !arg1 matching artifact.
Look for PRs that replace data_section_pad in grmaterial.c with identified data symbols.
Search for consumers of grMaterial_803E0A20 or gr_mobj to understand how this custom MObj info is used.
Check whether it_802E6AEC later receives typed parameters or a semantic name.
